title: Harness configuration
description: Configuration for the autonomy loop, the plan->execute->validate->review workflow, and the per-component verification gate. Tune per project; /harness-init seeds the stack-specific fields.

project.description: Whether this is a new or existing codebase. Greenfield and brownfield need different handling: greenfield establishes the stack/specs and tolerates higher autonomy; brownfield must first be understood and respected (mapped via /onboard), needs a green baseline before changes, and defaults to supervised with isolated, small-scope work.

type.description: greenfield = built from scratch; brownfield = pre-existing code the harness must not break.

baseline.description: The known-good starting point for a brownfield project. Established by /harness-init or /onboard: run the discovered gate and confirm it passes (or record which parts were already red). Without this, auto-rollback can't distinguish 'my change broke it' from 'it was broken before I started'.

ref.description: git commit the baseline gate was green at.

autonomy.description: How much rope the loop gets. Configurable from supervised to full-auto.

mode.description: supervised = pause at checkpoints for human y/n; auto = run unattended (Ralph-style) within the caps below.

maxTurnsPerIteration.description: Passed to `claude -p --max-turns` to hard-bound a single iteration's tool-call turns (so one iteration can't run away). Default 40.

tokenBudget.description: Per-run token cap. By default an ESTIMATE (claude -p text mode rarely reports counts, so a per-iteration estimate is used); set meterTokens=true to meter real usage from the model's JSON output (much closer, still approximate). Reset at each run start. The HARD runaway bounds are maxIterations + maxTurnsPerIteration. null = no cap.

meterTokens.description: When true, invoke the model with `--output-format json` so each iteration reports real `usage` (input+output tokens), making tokenBudget a much closer cap than the per-iteration estimate (parsed best-effort from the JSON, so still approximate). Trade-off: the per-iteration log is JSON rather than streamed text. Default false.

skipPermissions.description: Only honored when mode=auto. Passes --dangerously-skip-permissions for true unattended runs. DANGEROUS — safety rests on the gate + rollback + PreToolUse hook. Default false.

checkpoints.description: Where supervised mode pauses for human approval (ignored in auto mode).

planApproval.description: Pause after planning, before any code.

autoRollbackOnRed.description: git reset a failed iteration so the tree is never left broken.

workflow.description: The plan->execute->validate->review->record lifecycle that /work drives a task through. Each task in state/tasks.json carries a `status` that advances along these phases.

phases.description: Ordered phase names. The task `status` lifecycle is todo -> planned -> in_progress -> validated -> reviewed -> done.

checkpointBetweenPhases.description: In supervised mode, pause for human review between phases.

requireSprintContractBefore.description: Phase that may not start until a sprint contract (agreed definition of done) exists. Default 'execute'.

requireReviewBefore.description: Status a task may not reach until a fresh-context review has passed. Default 'done'.

components.description: The buildable units of the project. ONE entry for a single-root project (path '.'); MANY for a multi-repo layout (e.g. frontend/ + backend/), each with its own stack and gate. The harness runs each component's gate in that component's own directory. This is what makes the harness work for headless FE+BE projects.

path.description: Directory of this component relative to the repo root ('.' for a single-root project). Gate commands run with this as the working directory, and the edit-hook routes a changed file to the component whose path is its deepest prefix.

profile.description: Name of the stack profile in harness/profiles/ used to derive this component's gate.

run.description: How to run this component locally (dev server, entrypoint).

gate.description: Cross-cutting / integration gate, run from the repo ROOT after every component gate passes. Multi-component: usually only `e2e` (exercising the components together); single-component: leave entirely null.

freshContextReview.description: Require an independent review in a fresh context before merge. Doer != judge.

requireE2EEvidence.description: Require captured end-to-end evidence, not just passing unit tests.

reviewEveryNIterations.description: Wire the inferential judge into the unattended loop: every N GREEN (committed) iterations, spawn a fresh-context reviewer headless over the last N commits. On a REJECT verdict the loop records the finding to state/handoff.md and stops for a human. 0 = off (the loop runs the deterministic gate only — see ROADMAP). Requires loop.commitOnGreen.

failBelow.description: Sprint fails if any criterion scores below this hard threshold.

gate.description: A verification gate. Each value is a shell command (run in the relevant working directory) or null to skip that step. Execution order is fixed: format -> lint -> typecheck -> build -> test -> e2e. The harness defines WHEN; the stack profile defines WHAT. This is the seam that makes the harness tech-agnostic.

e2e.description: End-to-end / user-level check. Unit-green is not done.
